sources.notes: Official Round 1082 tutorial checked as the main source.
Used to confirm feasible range and construction.

annotation.summary: Construct a length-2n array containing each number 1..n exactly twice so the fixed recollection process finishes in exactly k operations, or report impossible.
annotation.constraints: The output itself has size 2n, so an O(n) construction is required.
annotation.core_idea: The number of operations is always between n and 2n-1. Let x = k - n + 1. Build a block on numbers 1..x that forces the maximum 2x-1 operations, then append each remaining value twice consecutively so each costs one operation. This gives (2x-1) + (n-x) = k.
tricks: split target operations into one maximal hard block plus easy adjacent pairs
construct an interleaving with maximal recollection cost

solution_variants.name: max-block-plus-pairs-construction
solution_variants.summary: Use the maximal-cost construction on the first x labels and adjacent duplicate pairs for the remaining labels.

tags.evidence: The task is solved by explicitly building a duplicate permutation realizing any feasible k.
The construction uses a maximal-cost block as an extreme building piece and fills the rest with minimum-cost pairs.
